title: rhamnus alaternus var. parvifolia
intro: Rhamnus alaternus var. parvifolia, also known as the Italian buckthorn, is a shrub-like plant in the family Rhamnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Italian buckthorn include its ability to support liver function, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Italian buckthorn can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine and herbalism.
